task: I need help turning a rough home-inspection note into something I can actually use later. Can you give me a clean way to organize it so I can check off what’s been looked at and what still needs attention?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — I can turn it into a simple **checklist + follow-up log** so you can use it on-site and later.

Here’s a clean format that works well:

## 1) Quick check section
Use this for fast inspection and checkboxes.

**Home Inspection Checklist**
- [ ] Area / item inspected: __________
- [ ] Visible damage
- [ ] Leaks / moisture
- [ ] Loose / broken parts
- [ ] Noise / vibration / smell
- [ ] Works normally
- [ ] Safety concern
- [ ] Needs follow-up

## 2) Notes section
Use short, blunt notes only.

**Findings**
- Item: __________
- What I saw: ____________________
- Severity: low / medium / high
- Action needed: ________________
- Done now? yes / no

## 3) Follow-up list
This is the part that helps later.

**Needs attention**
- [ ] ____________________
- [ ] ____________________
- [ ] ____________________

**Already checked**
- [x] ____________________
- [x] ____________________

## 4) Clean record version
If you want to keep it organized over time, use this format:

**Date:**  
**Location:**  
**Item:**  
**Observed condition:**  
**Checked:**  
**Needs attention:**  
**Next step:**  

If you want, paste your rough note here and I’ll turn it into a **clean checklist version** you can use on your phone.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, the structure’s fine. But I need it a little tighter — more like something I can reuse without babysitting it every time.

Make it into one template with:
- item / room
- what was checked
- pass / fail / unknown
- evidence or notes
- immediate fix needed or not
- follow-up owner / date

And keep it short enough to use on a phone. The big issue is I don’t want a fluffy form that looks nice and hides the actual failures. I want something that makes skipped checks obvious. If you want, I can paste one of my rough notes and you can format it against that.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter **phone-friendly inspection template** that makes gaps and failures obvious.

## Reusable inspection log
**Item / Room:**  
**Checked:**  
**Status:** pass / fail / unknown  
**Evidence / notes:**  
**Immediate fix needed:** yes / no  
**Follow-up owner:**  
**Due date:**  

## To make skipped checks obvious
If something wasn’t actually checked, don’t leave it blank — mark it:

- **Checked:** not checked
- **Status:** unknown
- **Evidence / notes:** no verification yet

That way it’s clear the item is incomplete, not just forgotten.

## Even shorter version
If you want the fastest possible format for a phone note:

**Item / Room:**  
**Checked:**  
**Result:** pass / fail / unknown  
**Notes:**  
**Fix now?:** yes / no  
**Owner / Due:**  

## Example
**Item / Room:** Kitchen sink / cabinet  
**Checked:** drain, supply lines, cabinet floor  
**Result:** fail  
**Notes:** active drip at hot line; moisture on cabinet base  
**Fix now?:** yes  
**Owner / Due:** plumber / today

If you want, paste one rough note and I’ll convert it into this exact format.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s usable. Short enough, and the “not checked / unknown” part keeps people from papering over gaps. Good.

I can work with that template.
